question_text: A light bulb converts 60J of electrical energy into 15J of light energy and 45J of heat energy. Calculate the efficiency of the light bulb.
correct_answer: Efficiency = 25% (or 0.25). This is calculated as: useful energy output (15J) ÷ total energy input (60J) × 100 = 25%
explanation: Efficiency is calculated by dividing the useful energy output (light) by the total energy input, then multiplying by 100 to get a percentage. Only the light (15J) is useful; the heat (45J) is wasted energy.
